title: Barak entered the building first and was killed: The danger of IEDs and the understanding - the ceasefire terms are worse this time
content: Two days have passed since the beginning of the ceasefire in Lebanon, and it is already clear that its terms are worse than those agreed upon after Operation Northern Arrows at the end of 2024. The main concern in the IDF is from IED zones that were laid before the ceasefire. From such a charge in the village of Jabin, reserve paratrooper Barak Calphon (48), a resident of the settlement of Adi in the Jezreel Valley, was killed. Three other fighters were injured in the incident. Calphon was the first to enter with the company commander into the building, and during the searches, the IED placed by Hezbollah exploded. Defense Minister Israel Katz eulogized Calphon and said: 'Barak was a brave and dedicated fighter and a model family man.' The forces in southern Lebanon continue to clear the areas, and the IDF has divided the area into three lines: the Red Line, the Yellow Line, and the Litani Line.
